serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one fried pie piece, approximately 85 g, as served at McDonald's Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Lychee Nata de Coco Pie — Nutrition Information; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Fried Fruit Pie (SR Legacy #18302) and USDA Branded Food analogues for fried pastry desserts
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Branded Food) — used as primary nutrient anchor for fried fruit pie category; McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel used for serving size and product context
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Lychee Nata de Coco Pie exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry 18302 (Pie, fried, fruit) which represents a fried pastry shell with sweet fruit filling, closely analogous in composition. Per-100g values are derived from this reference. Lychee filling and nata de coco (coconut gel) contribute primarily carbohydrates and sugar with negligible fat and protein, consistent with the fried fruit pie profile. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual product values may vary by batch.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on fried pastry / sweet pie category
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lychee Nata de Coco Pie. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous category for sweet fried pastry / fruit pie products (GI range 60–70 reported for similar fried dough-based sweet pastries). Lychee has a GI of approximately 57 (low-medium) and nata de coco is a low-GI coconut gel; however, the dominant GI driver is the fried wheat-flour pastry shell. GI 65 (medium) is a defensible central estimate. GL per serving = (65 × 32.7 g available carbohydrate per 85 g serving) / 100 = 21.3, rounded to 21.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg per 100 g; approximately 179 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit consumption of this fried pie as one serving contributes approximately 179 mg sodium, and the high refined-carbohydrate and fat load may further stress cardiovascular regulation; occasional treat only.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (65) combined with high sugar content (16 g per 100 g) and refined carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this item as an occasional indulgence only; one serving delivers approximately 27.8 g available carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~21, which will produce a significant postprandial glucose rise and should be accounted for in daily carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (5.8 g per 100 g) and trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g) from deep-frying in partially hydrogenated or palm-based oils
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this deep-fried pastry due to its saturated fat and potential trans fat content, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; consumption should be infrequent and portion-controlled.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Nata de Coco Pie is a deep-fried sweet pastry dessert sold at McDonald's Singapore as a limited or seasonal menu item. The pie consists of a crispy, golden fried wheat-flour pastry shell encasing a sweet filling made from lychee fruit and nata de coco — a chewy, translucent coconut gel produced by bacterial fermentation of coconut water. The lychee filling provides a floral, mildly sweet tropical flavour, while the nata de coco contributes a distinctive bouncy, jelly-like texture that contrasts with the crisp pastry exterior. The product is analogous in format to McDonald's classic fried apple pie but uses Southeast Asian tropical ingredients. Nutritionally, one pie (approximately 85 g) delivers around 242 kcal, 32.7 g carbohydrate, 11.5 g fat, and 16 g sugar per serving, making it a high-sugar, moderate-fat dessert item. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), driven primarily by the refined wheat-flour pastry shell. Clinically, this item is a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its medium GI and significant sugar load, and a moderate concern for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat content. It is halal-certified and suitable for vegetarians. The product is not suitable for those with gluten intolerance. It is best consumed as an occasional treat within a balanced diet.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Lychee Nata de Coco Pie exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry 18302 (Fried Fruit Pie), which is the closest defensible analogue. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for fried sweet pastries. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy #18302 (Pie, fried, fruit) as no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore product. Actual product values may differ. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference entry for fried fruit pie. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as this is a plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients reported; value not reported in source. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy reference; this fried fruit pie uses vegetable oil and contains no animal-derived filling ingredients. 10. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on fried sweet pastry category from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this product. 11. The cuisine tag 'Burger' in the input record reflects the field survey platform category for McDonald's and does not accurately describe this dessert item; reclassified as 'Fast Food / Western' with food_group 'Desserts'. 12. Halal status preserved from input cuisine_tags. 13. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-served fast food item.
